skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Create hosted AI videos through the Image Skill CLI and API. The skill guides agents through model choice, no-spend checks, job creation, cost limits, hosted URLs, and feedback for video generation tasks.
skill.summary: Generate hosted AI videos with Image Skill CLI guidance, cost checks, and recoverable job URLs.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly Markdown command and URL detections, not confirmed malware. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs agents to run remote npm packages, contact a hosted API, use an optional service token, and create paid hosted media jobs.

medium_findings.title: Remote npm Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill provides commands that run image-skill and skills packages through npx, including the latest package tag. This is legitimate for a CLI-based hosted video workflow, but it creates supply-chain and command execution risk if users run it without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly contains runnable npx commands. The commands support the stated skill purpose, but remote package execution remains a real security consideration.

medium_findings.title: Hosted API, Token, and Spend Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill points agents to image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com and describes an optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN plus credit and payment loops. This is expected for hosted media generation, but it introduces network, privacy, account, and spending considerations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hosted service URLs, token name, and payment language are visible in SKILL.md. No evidence shows exfiltration or hidden endpoints, so the concern is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Workspace Profile Directory Writes
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The fallback install instructions set HOME and CODEX_HOME to workspace-local hidden directories and create them with mkdir. This is a scoped filesystem change, but users should understand that agent profile state may be written under the current workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly sets HOME and CODEX_HOME and creates those directories. The commands are transparent and local, which lowers severity.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts correspond to Markdown inline code and fenced command examples, not Ruby backtick execution in a program. The file contains documentation only, with no Ruby source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears in Markdown prose or bash code fences. There is no Ruby file or executable Ruby expression in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-severity weak crypto alerts on lines 3 and 19 do not show a cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption implementation. They appear to be matches against ordinary description and token text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found front matter description text and an environment variable description only. No cryptographic code or algorithm selection is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Remote Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to run remote npm packages through npx, including image-skill@latest and skills add commands. Pinning versions and reviewing package provenance would reduce supply-chain risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the Markdown file. They are intentional workflow commands, but they execute external packages.

dangerous_patterns.title: Paid Hosted Media Generation Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill explains cost limits, credit balance, wallet, payment loops, and hosted media URLs. This is not malicious, but agents should require explicit spend approval and avoid sensitive prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow text explicitly references costs, payment loops, and media spend. The guide includes no-spend checks, which reduces but does not remove operational risk.

content.value_statement: AI video requests often require provider setup, billing, and job tracking. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through hosted Image Skill video creation with cost checks and recoverable URLs.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to start with a no-spend video generation check.
Provides example commands for creating hosted AI video jobs through Image Skill.
Documents the fal.ltx-video-13b-distilled model as the default video option.
Explains how to preserve generated work as durable hosted media URLs.
Includes installation commands for Codex-compatible skill environments.
Directs agents to submit feedback when required models or capabilities are missing.
limitations: It depends on the external Image Skill service and npm packages.
It does not generate video locally or include model weights.
Paid creation should only run after explicit media spend approval.
The skill file does not define detailed provider policy checks.

use_cases.title: Prototype product motion concepts
use_cases.description: Create short hosted product reveal videos from concise prompts with budget limits.

use_cases.title: Generate campaign video drafts
use_cases.description: Turn marketing ideas into reviewable hosted video clips without setting up separate provider accounts.

use_cases.title: Automate agent media workflows
use_cases.description: Use repeatable CLI guidance for model selection, job recovery, cost receipts, and feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple video prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first hosted clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AI video generation skill to draft a safe no-spend plan for a ten second product reveal video. Show the prompt you would use before creating anything.

prompt_templates.title: Compare prompt options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to prepare three text-to-video prompt options for a social media teaser. Include motion, subject, style, and estimated spend controls.

prompt_templates.title: Run a controlled generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Image Skill for a hosted AI video job after confirming spend is allowed. Keep the cost cap low and return the hosted result URL and receipt summary.

prompt_templates.title: Recover and refine a video workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to recover the last hosted video job, assess whether the model met the brief, and prepare feedback for missing capability or latency issues.

output_examples.input: Create a handheld product reveal for a new desk lamp.
output_examples.output: The agent prepares a no-spend guide, confirms the prompt and budget limit, then returns a hosted video URL after approval.

output_examples.input: Make a short animated concept for a launch announcement.
output_examples.output: The agent proposes prompt variants, identifies the selected video model, and explains the cost control before generation.

output_examples.input: The video model is missing the feature I need.
output_examples.output: The agent documents the attempted command, expected behavior, actual behavior, and missing capability for Image Skill feedback.

best_practices: Start with the no-spend guide before creating paid media.
Use explicit cost caps and require approval before spending credits.
Avoid sensitive, private, or regulated content in prompts sent to hosted services.
anti_patterns: Do not run remote npm commands without reviewing the package source and command arguments.
Do not include provider credentials or private business data in video prompts.
Do not bypass the guide when budget, model, or policy requirements are unclear.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the hosted Image Skill runtime and CLI guidance for video generation jobs.

faq.question: Does it require provider API keys?
faq.answer: The skill says provider API keys are not required. It may use an optional Image Skill token.

faq.question: Can it spend money automatically?
faq.answer: It includes cost and payment workflow guidance. Agents should require explicit approval before any paid creation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What video model is shown in the examples?
faq.answer: The examples use fal.ltx-video-13b-distilled as the video generation model.

faq.question: What should happen if a model or feature is missing?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to use fallback only for the gap and send structured feedback to Image Skill.
